NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE METEOROLOGISTS SURVEYED DAMAGE IN AND AROUND THE BROOKHAVEN SUBDIVISION AND DETERMINED IT TO BE THE RESULT OF STRAIGHT-LINE WINDS. THE DAMAGE BEGAN ALONG OLD ACTON RD WHERE A TREE WAS UPROOTED AND ONE HOME SUSTAINED MINOR FASCIA DAMAGE. THE WIND DAMAGE CONTINUED EAST-NORTHEAST INTO THE BROOKHAVEN SUBIDIVISION WHERE SCATTERED SHINGLE DAMAGE WAS NOTED AND TREES WERE UPROOTED IN SEVERAL LOCATIONS. MAXIMUM WIND SPEEDS WERE ESTIMATED TO BE AROUND 65 MPH.

NWS METEOROLOGISTS DETERMINED THAT AN EF-0 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN ST. CLAIR COUNTY WITH MAXIMUM WINDS OF 85 MPH, AND WIDTH OF 150 YDS. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ON JAMAICA LANE, MOVING NORTHEAST THROUGH THE TOWN OF BRANCHVILLE. HOMES SUSTAINED MINOR DAMAGE ON SANIE ROAD AND NELSIE ANN DRIVE, AS WELL AS HURST DRIVE AND SHADOW BEND RD. THE TORNADO CROSSED HIGHWAY 411 IN BRANCHVILLE AND LIFTED JUST NORTHEAST OF TUCKER ROAD. ABOUT 150 TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED ALONG THE PATH.
TREE DOWN BLOCKING BUYCK RD 2 MILES FROM HWY 231. WINDS EST AT 40 MPH.

A SHORT-LIVED TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN IN A WOODED AREA NEAR W.L. EZELL ROAD IN THE SILOAM COMMUNITY. THE TORNADO TRAVELED TO THE NORTHEAST...CROSSING AL-17 AND DISSIPATED JUST SOUTH OF U.S. HIGHWAY 80. DAMAGE WAS CONSISTENT WITH AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH WINDS OF 100 MPH. ALONG W.L. EZELL ROAD AND COUNTY ROAD 9...TWO WELL-BUILT HOMES RECEIVED SIGNIFICANT ROOF AND OTHER STRUCTURAL DAMAGE. NINE OTHER HOMES RECEIVED ROOF DAMAGE...AND SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS WERE DESTROYED.

*** 13 INJ *** A TORNADO WITH MAX WINDS OF 100MPH TOUCHED DOWN 0.5 MI WEST OF HWY 69 DOWNING HUNDREDS OF TREES AND CAUSING MINOR SHINGLE DAMAGE ACROSS MULTIPLE SUBDIVISIONS, INCLUDING SEVERAL LARGE AND VERY OLD HARDWOODS AT LITTLE SANDY BAPTIST CHURCH. A TORNADO BEGAN ABOUT 2.4 MILES EAST-SOUTHEAST OF FORKLAND...JUST SOUTH OF COUNTY ROAD 20 NEAR THE NORTHERN EDGE OF CATFISH PONDS...WHERE A PERSON OBSERVED THE TORNADO. FROM THERE IT MOVED NORTH-NORTHEAST AND ENDED NEAR LLOYD CHAPEL CHURCH ALONG COUNTY ROAD 41. SEVERAL TREES WERE EITHER SNAPPED OR UPROOTED ALONG THE PATH.
TREES DOWN NEAR LAKEPOINT RESORT. POSSIBLE TORNADO. PER SOCIAL MEDIA.

THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ABOUT 1.8 MILES SOUTHEAST OF LAKEPOINT RESORT STATE PARK...NEAR WHERE COWICKEE CREEK EMPTIES INTO WALTER F GEORGE RESERVOIR. FROM THERE IT MOVED NORTHEASTWARD OVER WOODED SWAMPLAND...AND EVENTUALLY CROSSED THE RESERVOIR AND MOVED INTO GEORGIA ROUGHLY DUE EAST OF THE STATE PARK. HUNDREDS OF TREES WERE SNAPPED OFF AND UPROOTED. THIS TORNADO CONTINUED INTO STEWART COUNTY GEORGIA.
DAMAGE TO A METAL BUILDING AND TREE LIMBS DOWN IN CRAWFORD NEAR HWY 80. POSSIBLE TORNADO. PER SOCIAL MEDIA.
